stem: Triangle GHI is similar to triangle JKL, where angle G corresponds to angle J, and angle H corresponds to angle K. The measure of angle J is 128 degrees, and the measure of angle K is x degrees. If the measure of angle I is 35 degrees, what is the value of x?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. Because the triangles are similar, the measure of angle G equals the measure of angle J = 128 degrees. The interior angles of triangle GHI sum to 180 degrees, so the measure of angle H is 180 minus 128 minus 35 = 17 degrees. By similarity, angle K has the same measure as angle H, so x = 17.

Choice A is incorrect: 35 is the measure of angle I.

Choice B is incorrect: 128 is the measure of angle J.

Choice C is incorrect: 52 is the sum of the other two angles.
